title: The Corpse Sniffing Dog

jokes.text: When did you start losing it? I started losing when I was 15. 15, wow, earlier than me, yeah.
jokes.setup: Two men meeting for a job interview
jokes.punchline: Immediately bond over baldness timing like it's a competition
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David - turning a professional interaction into an uncomfortable personal bonding moment. Character-driven and believable.

jokes.text: No convertibles. Oh, God, I hate that. Oh, absolutely. 'Cause you have to wear a hat if you're gonna be in a convertible, and then you look like you're trying to hide something.
jokes.setup: Discussion of bald guy problems with sunscreen
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about convertibles creating a catch-22 with hats
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy that feels authentic to the characters' shared experience.

jokes.text: They wear it all the time, and they'll meet a girl then they'll show up on a date. Are they gonna take the hat off? Wear it? They have a terrible decision... Then the girl is gonna be, like, 'I didn't know you were bald.' 'You misrepresented yourself.' 'You're a liar.'
jokes.setup: Hat-wearing bald guys trying to hide their baldness
jokes.punchline: The escalation from deception to being called a liar over baldness
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation that builds the tension of the dating deception scenario.

jokes.text: I'm surprised Hitler didn't round up the toupee people. If I'm going to be a sick megalomaniac, to round up people who I hated, they would be on my list.
jokes.setup: Conversation about hating toupee wearers
jokes.punchline: Hitler comparison taken to logical extreme with personal megalomaniac fantasy
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David - taking a petty complaint to historically dark extremes. Shocking but character-consistent.

jokes.text: I would say, 'Get the toupee people.' Absolutely. I'd have my henchmen going around tugging at people's hair.
jokes.setup: Hitler/megalomaniac fantasy about toupee wearers
jokes.punchline: Specific image of henchmen tugging at hair to identify victims
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation with vivid physical comedy image that makes the absurd scenario concrete.

jokes.text: If it comes off... 'Balden,' come with me. Ach, 'Balden.'
jokes.setup: Hair-tugging test for toupee wearers
jokes.punchline: German accent saying 'Balden' (bald one) as if rounding up victims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining 'bald' with German concentration camp imagery. Dark, economical, and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Ah-hh! Does that bother anybody when I make a noise like that? Is anybody disturbed by that? And why should you be? What is it? So what?
jokes.setup: Larry makes an odd noise at dinner
jokes.punchline: Over-explains and justifies the noise, making it more awkward
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry move - making a minor social moment into a bigger issue through over-analysis.

jokes.text: It's a corpse-sniffing dog. They got it from the fire department.
jokes.setup: Describing a good, smart, trained dog
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's specifically trained to find dead bodies
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expectations. Sets up premise for entire episode with absurd specificity.

jokes.text: It's like having a person there who barks.
jokes.setup: Describing how smart the dog is
jokes.punchline: Reduces human intelligence to just barking
jokes.explanation: Economical and absurd way to describe dog intelligence that lands well.

jokes.text: He's like an elephant sticking his head in a pond. Why are you watching this? Ugh... this routine. Stop it. Um-um. No, I'm sorry, no.
jokes.setup: Larry watching something on TV
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's disgusted reaction and Larry's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Domestic cringe moment showing Larry's questionable viewing habits and Cheryl's reaction.

jokes.text: The cork is dry. We need to send this back. Don't send that back. Feel the cork. I don't give a shit. You don't even have to taste it.
jokes.setup: Larry examining wine at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Stu's blunt rejection of Larry's wine expertise
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for Larry's pretentiousness being shut down by practical friend.

jokes.text: At $140 a bottle, I don't want 'fine.' 'Larry couldn't possibly tell... No, Larry, Larry, come on, you can't. Larry's not sophisticated enough to know...'
jokes.setup: Being told the wine is fine
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid projection of others questioning his sophistication
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's insecurity and tendency to imagine slights. Character-driven escalation.

jokes.text: I'll get you a nice spritzer. A spritzer.
jokes.setup: Larry being difficult about wine
jokes.punchline: Stu's condescending suggestion of a spritzer
jokes.explanation: Effective putdown delivered deadpan. Spritzer suggestion is perfectly emasculating.

jokes.text: When I was on 'Seinfeld' I wasn't allowed to talk about 'Seinfeld.' I'm not allowed to talk about anything I'm doing. I bore her, she bores so easily.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he can't talk about his restaurant
jokes.punchline: The parallel between Seinfeld and current situation, plus wife criticism
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Larry's own show while developing character relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: Larry only likes him because he's bald. That is so... No, that's true. What makes you say that, Cheryl? He told me. He goes, 'I found this guy, he's great. He's bald.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of new chef
jokes.punchline: Cheryl revealing Larry's bald-based hiring preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to opening scene while revealing Larry's biases. Character-driven and quotable.

jokes.text: I did hire a bald man, but... And only because he's bald. No, no, no.
jokes.setup: Larry being accused of bald-based hiring
jokes.punchline: His weak denial that actually confirms it
jokes.explanation: Larry's inability to convincingly deny his obvious bias. Structure works well.

jokes.text: Why... why do I have to thank you?
jokes.setup: Susan expecting thanks for dinner
jokes.punchline: Larry's genuine confusion about thanking her
jokes.explanation: Sets up major conflict of episode. Larry's social obliviousness drives the cringe.

jokes.text: Well, you can call it our money, but for the sake of discussion, he's the one who goes to work and earns the money. You don't work.
jokes.setup: Argument about whose money paid for dinner
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt statement about Susan not working
jokes.explanation: Escalates the conflict with Larry's tactless honesty about traditional gender roles.

jokes.text: Loving you is my job. I earned the money. It's just a fact.
jokes.setup: Cheryl trying to defuse with romantic comment
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt factual response ignoring the romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - completely missing emotional cues for literal facts. Well-structured contrast.

jokes.text: Hey, you know me, I don't like to complain. Oh, yeah. I think you know me well enough to know that by now.
jokes.setup: Larry about to complain about something
jokes.punchline: Claims he doesn't like to complain (while constantly complaining)
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic setup showing Larry's complete lack of self-awareness about his complaining nature.

jokes.text: We want a little pee privacy, do we not? Yeah, privacy. I'm absolutely with you. You don't want someone looking down at your thing.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about bathroom partitions
jokes.punchline: Blunt discussion of urinal privacy concerns
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry observation about male bathroom etiquette. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: This is not gonna be that kind of crowd. This is a high-class restaurant, Larry. Still people go off to the side. They've got the 45° angle things.
jokes.setup: Michael dismissing need for urinal partitions
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific knowledge of urinal positioning strategies
jokes.explanation: Continues the urinal discussion with Larry's overthinking of bathroom logistics.

jokes.text: My family's dog, not my dog. This dog kills me, I'm so allergic to it. I have to hang with it today 'cause Susie's got some stuff to do with tiling at the new house.
jokes.setup: Jeff brings dog to restaurant
jokes.punchline: He's severely allergic but stuck with it anyway
jokes.explanation: Sets up Jeff's predicament while showing typical married man compromise situation.

jokes.text: What's he got? Did somebody drop some meat or something? No, that floor is spotless for the health inspection. Looks like he smells something.
jokes.setup: Dog acting strange in restaurant kitchen
jokes.punchline: Building suspense about what the corpse-sniffing dog found
jokes.explanation: Good tension building as the episode's central premise comes into play.

jokes.text: Isn't he a corpse-sniffing dog?
jokes.setup: Dog acting strange and sniffing around
jokes.punchline: Larry realizes the horrifying implication
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that transforms setup into nightmare scenario. Economy and timing excellent.

jokes.text: There will have to be an investigation, and we'll have to tear up the floor. Oh, come on that's ridiculous! How long will that take? I don't know. Oh, this is a nightmare.
jokes.setup: Dog detected something under restaurant floor
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic nightmare that will destroy restaurant opening
jokes.explanation: Escalates the central conflict with bureaucratic absurdity. Classic Curb situation spiraling.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, I'm gonna take my money out of this place. What if there's a dead body under there? We're fucked. No one will ever come to the restaurant where they found a body.
jokes.setup: Restaurant investigation underway
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate panic and business catastrophizing
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's immediate self-interest and panic. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: We're fucked. No one will ever come to the restaurant where they found a body. It'll be in the papers. We're fucked. If there's a dead body underneath there, we are fucked.
jokes.setup: Potential corpse discovery
jokes.punchline: Michael's panicked repetition of 'we're fucked'
jokes.explanation: Repetitive and not particularly clever. More plot advancement than comedy.

jokes.text: You know the Braudys? That guy who smashed into Alanis Morissette? Oh, that idiot. Yeah, that guy.
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning potential dog adopters
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity car accident as identifying characteristic
jokes.explanation: Typical Curb random celebrity reference that feels authentic to how people actually talk.

jokes.text: It's nice to be affectionate to something German. You don't get the opportunity that often, you know?
jokes.setup: Larry petting the German shepherd
jokes.punchline: Comment about rare opportunities for German affection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to earlier Hitler jokes with perfectly dark Larry logic. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Mmm... Oscar. What I'm saying is, if you say Oscar, Daddy won't be here. I know. You know? Mm-hmm. But you're choosing Oscar. I'm your dad. I just love that dog.
jokes.setup: Jeff giving his daughter ultimatum between him and dog
jokes.punchline: She immediately chooses the dog without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Devastating comedic moment - child's brutal honesty destroys father. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: What does he got there? Holy shit. What do you got there? A bra? Is that it? Is there more?
jokes.setup: Tension about what corpse-sniffing dog found under floor
jokes.punchline: Just a bra - complete anticlimax
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and anticlimax. All the buildup for just a bra. Excellent structure and timing.

jokes.text: He's a bra-sniffing dog. He's a good bra-sniffing dog.
jokes.setup: Revelation that dog found bra, not corpse
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact reclassification of the dog
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that reframes entire episode premise. Economical and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: can we get the splash guards in the urinal now? What do you think? I'll have time for that. That's good, Larry. We'll get the splash guards. Blessing in disguise.
jokes.setup: Restaurant destroyed by investigation
jokes.punchline: Larry sees opportunity to finally get bathroom partitions
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Larry's ability to find silver lining in any disaster. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: A dog! She chose a fucking dog over her own father. You sat down, you laid it out? I told her, 'Daddy's sick. He can't stay in the same house with Oscar.' She wants Oscar! She wants the dog!
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining daughter chose dog over him
jokes.punchline: His incredulous repetition and escalating outrage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jeff's outrage at being rejected by his daughter for a dog.

jokes.text: A bra? What the fuck's wrong with that dog? He's a bra-sniffing dog. A bra-sniffing dog? What the fuck?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the bra discovery
jokes.punchline: Jeff's incredulous reaction to bra-sniffing revelation
jokes.explanation: Continues the bra-sniffing dog revelation with appropriate outrage from Jeff.

jokes.text: Believe me, I know as much about this as the next guy. Come on. No. Too fruity. And a tad on the oaky side. What does 'oaky' mean? It tastes a little like a tree.
jokes.setup: Larry trying wine at Jeff's house
jokes.punchline: Pretentious wine critique followed by childish explanation to 7-year-old
jokes.explanation: Great callback to wine snobbery with added cringe of explaining it to a child.

jokes.text: Put him in a cage and you can torture him a little bit. They're boring. But you torture them. It's not boring if you torture them.
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting hamster as pet alternative
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting torture as entertainment for child
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and wrong advice to give a child. Peak Larry inappropriateness with perfect delivery.

jokes.text: I don't want Oscar. I want Daddy.
jokes.setup: Larry's manipulation attempt with wine and conversation
jokes.punchline: Child reverses her position after Larry's 'help'
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal that shows Larry's influence working, but now creates new problem.

jokes.text: I enjoyed the chicken very much. I was surprised at how good it was, actually. Very funny, that's very funny.
jokes.setup: Susan expecting apology for dinner argument
jokes.punchline: Larry completely misses point and compliments food instead
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Larry's complete social obliviousness to what's expected.

jokes.text: I guess you'll have to continue to wait if it's a thank you that you're waiting for. Because a thank you is just a little more than you can handle.
jokes.setup: Susan waiting for thank you
jokes.punchline: Larry's stubborn refusal and accusation she can't handle it
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's stubbornness escalating into insult. Character-driven conflict.

jokes.text: Because you haven't worked a day since you got out of college. You've never held a job... Hey, look it's a dog. Doggie! Oh, he's so cute.
jokes.setup: Larry's harsh criticism of Susan's work history
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent interruption about the dog
jokes.explanation: Good tension break with innocent child interruption during adult conflict.

jokes.text: She's slurring her words, she's bumping into things, she stinks like a fuckin' wino! Oh, I poured some of your... I had poured some wine. Oh, she must have accidentally... a seven-year-old kid drank some wine?
jokes.setup: Jeff discovering his daughter is drunk
jokes.punchline: Larry's weak explanation about accidental wine consumption
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing Larry accidentally got a child drunk. Genuinely shocking development.

jokes.text: I thought she had a speech impediment. You've known the kid since she was born, and she suddenly develops a speech impediment? That's what was so puzzling to me.
jokes.setup: Jeff pointing out Larry's absurd excuse
jokes.punchline: Larry doubling down on the speech impediment theory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - he genuinely thought the slurring was a speech impediment. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: I don't know what got into me. I was so wrong about that. You're married, you're a couple. of course you get thanked. It's a 50/50 thing. So what if he works and you don't? That has nothing to do with it.
jokes.setup: Larry appearing to genuinely apologize
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of his earlier position
jokes.explanation: Seems like genuine growth but we know Larry has ulterior motives for the apology.

jokes.text: Well, it's a long story, but when her daughter told me I could have the dog, she was quite drunk at the time, unbeknownst to me. Drunk? How old is this girl? Seven.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he needs dog back
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about getting seven-year-old drunk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant casual reveal of the drunk child story. Larry's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh my God, I just knew it. And as long as I'm here, I'll take the dog. As long as you happen to be here, you'll take the dog back?
jokes.setup: Larry's transparent manipulation with fake apology
jokes.punchline: Susan immediately sees through his scheme
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Larry's manipulation was transparent. Susan's not fooled.

jokes.text: Well, Larry, go back and tell her to have another drink. Because she's not getting her dog back, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry asking for dog back for drunk child
jokes.punchline: Susan's sarcastic suggestion to give child another drink
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comeback referencing the drunk child situation. Sharp and memorable.

jokes.text: Oscar! You...! Oscar! Oscar, you come back right...! Larry, you piece of shit! Thank you, thank you very much!
jokes.setup: Dog escapes back to Larry during argument
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic thanks as he steals dog through circumstance
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending escalation with ironic callback to the thank you theme. Dog choosing Larry completes the chaos.

jokes.text: Why'd you bring the dog back?
jokes.setup: Larry returning with the dog after all the chaos
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's exhausted question implying Larry failed at everything
jokes.explanation: Solid ending beat showing Larry's complete failure. Cheryl's delivery would sell the exhaustion.

tmdb_overview: Over a makeup dinner with the Braudys, Larry openly ponders the question, ""When a husband pays the check, do you have to also thank the wife?"" Meanwhile, Jeff's return home is ruined by his allergy to the family's corpse-sniffing German Shepherdâ€”but his daughter Sammy refuses to give away the dog. Concerned about his agent's welfare, Larry figures out an ingenious way to give the pooch to the Braudys, who are looking for just such a dog.
